pages.text: Ann-Nem-Oh-Nee lived in the ocean. All day long she clung to the same slippery rock with her single big foot. Her tiny tentacles caught tasty treats.

blocks.text: Ann-Nem-Oh-Nee lived in the ocean. All day long she clung to the same slippery rock with her single big foot. Her tiny tentacles caught tasty treats.

pages.text: On days when she felt cross, her stinging tentacles kept the other creatures away.

blocks.text: On days when she felt cross, her stinging tentacles kept the other creatures away.

pages.text: But today her rock felt lonely.

blocks.text: But today her rock felt lonely.

pages.text: “I have no fins. I have no feet. I am different to every other creature.”

blocks.text: “I have no fins.
I have no feet.
I am different to every other creature.”

pages.text: “Even that hermit crab is off having adventures!” “Save me! That fish wants to pluck me out of my shell!”

blocks.text: “Even that hermit crab is off having adventures!”
“Save me! That fish wants to pluck me out of my shell!”

pages.text: Ann-Nem-Oh-Nee whipped up her stinging tentacles and hid the hermit underneath them. “Go away, you big bully!” The big fish bared his teeth, but was afraid of being stung.

blocks.text: Ann-Nem-Oh-Nee whipped up her stinging tentacles and hid the hermit underneath them.
“Go away, you big bully!”
The big fish bared his teeth, but was afraid of being stung.

pages.text: He gave up and swam away with a cheeky flick of his tail. “That was close!” said the crab. “Is it safe now?” “You can come out, Crab. What’s your name?” “Herman. I’m a hermit, but I think I need a bodyguard. I wish I was as brave as you!”

blocks.text: He gave up and swam away with a cheeky flick of his tail.
“That was close!” said the crab. “Is it safe now?”
“You can come out, Crab. What’s your name?”
“Herman. I’m a hermit, but I think I need a bodyguard. I wish I was as brave as you!”

pages.text: “I’m Ann-Nem-Oh-Nee. I wish I had amazing adventures like you.” “But why don’t you?” He asked. “I’m stuck to my rock. And I don’t have legs!” She replied. “Well, I don’t have stings, but I do have an idea!”

blocks.text: “I’m Ann-Nem-Oh-Nee. I wish I had amazing adventures like you.”
“But why don’t you?” He asked.
“I’m stuck to my rock. And I don’t have legs!” She replied.
“Well, I don’t have stings, but I do have an idea!”

pages.text: Herman took Ann-Nem-Oh-Nee by the tentacle and danced around her, pulling silly faces. First Ann-Nem-Oh-Nee snorted, and then she giggled — and then she laughed so hard she popped right off her rock.

blocks.text: Herman took Ann-Nem-Oh-Nee by the tentacle and danced around her, pulling silly faces.
First Ann-Nem-Oh-Nee snorted, and then she giggled — and then she laughed so hard she popped right off her rock.

pages.text: Herman gently picked her up and settled her on his shell. “Are you ready? I feel an adventure coming on.” He said.

blocks.text: Herman gently picked her up and settled her on his shell.
“Are you ready? I feel an adventure coming on.” He said.

pages.text: Ann-Nem-Oh-Nee adventures all over the ocean, catching tasty treats. Now she only uses her stinging tentacles to keep the nasty fish away from Herman.

blocks.text: Ann-Nem-Oh-Nee adventures all over the ocean, catching tasty treats.
Now she only uses her stinging tentacles to keep the nasty fish away from Herman.

pages.text: And she never, ever feels lonely.

blocks.text: And she never, ever feels lonely.
